title: Silky Russian Chickpea Flour Stir-fry
description: This fermented and silky chickpea flour dish is a vegetarian-friendly solo-dinner option that combines the umami flavors of fermented soy sauce with aromatic spices for a perfect fusion of textures and tastes.

ingredients.name: 3 tablespoons chickpea flour
ingredients.notes: Use high-quality, freshly made from fermented chickpeas.

ingredients.name: 2 large eggs (lightly beaten)
ingredients.notes: For a smooth texture and binding agent.

ingredients.name: 1 small onion, finely chopped
ingredients.notes: Add more for flavor enhancement if desired.

ingredients.name: 1 red bell pepper, diced (optional)
ingredients.notes: To add color and sweetness to the dish.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup fresh dill leaves, finely chopped
ingredients.notes: For a pop of freshness and color.

ingredients.name: 1 tbsp vinegar (rice or apple cider)
ingredients.notes: To balance the umami flavors with acidity.

instructions: Mix chickpea flour, eggs, salt, and vinegar in a large bowl until smooth and no lumps remain. If using onion and red bell pepper, add them along with the minced garlic to the batter.

Heat vegetable oil in a non-stick wok or skillet over medium heat. Pour the chickpea flour mixture into the pan and stir constantly for about 5 minutes or until it forms large crumbled pieces that are golden brown on the outside.
If using onion, red bell pepper, and dill, sauté them in a separate pan with some oil until softened, then mix them into the chickpea flour mixture. Toss everything together to combine thoroughly.
Serve hot as a main dish or side dish with steamed rice or noodles.
